pull.title: bench: Add more realistic Coin Selection Bench
pull.body: Adds a Coin Selection benchmark that doesn’t just test a worst case of one of the algorithms but exercises coin selection to to select inputs for a variety of different targets from a large number of UTXOs.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/33160. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | ACK | [l0rinc](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33160#issuecomment-4383518557) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore. ### Conflicts Reviewers, this pull request conflicts with the following ones: * [#33034](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33034) (wallet: Store transactions in a separate sqlite table by achow101) * [#33032](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33032) (wallet, test: Replace MockableDatabase with in-memory SQLiteDatabase by achow101) If you consider this pull request important, please also help to review the conflicting pull requests. Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first.

Took almost all of your suggestion (unresolved where I didn’t or had comments).

Spent more time staring at it, and was left wondering what the old Coin Selection benchmark was adding at this point, so I instead restructured the PR to replace it.

The current version is a lot simplers.
I think we could make it more reliable by resetting the mutations inside the measurement block with the new [setup](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34208) method.

events.body: Thanks for the review and great suggestions, @l0rinc. I took all of your suggestions and rebased. Should be ready for review again.

code review ACK 0429c503fb0500e425e9a4c8251606128e66872f

Thanks for taking my suggestions, lgtm.

comments.body: we don't actually need the wallet here, right?

we want deterministic randomness for benchmarks, otherwise it's hard to know what we're actually measuring:
```suggestion
    FastRandomContext rand{/*fDeterministic=*/true};
```
I'd avoid the `Narrow No-Break Space` chars from docs if possible, they're rendered differently on different mediums

Same, seems simpler to just leave it to deterministic instead of hard-coding a confusing seed
```suggestion
    FastRandomContext det_rand{/*fDeterministic=*/true};
```

note: can we reuse this for the random source below as well?

Please rebase, `spendable` was removed since: https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/commit/6a7aa015747e2634fe5a4b2f7fa0d104eb75c796#diff-38f1a8db124a979cb6dd76ce263f7aae0053d6967ee909e6356115fa0402dc8cL78
`nInput` should be `uint32_t` otherwise we'd have a narrowing conversion in `OutPoint{tx.GetHash(), nInput}`
we don't have to keep this comment either in 2dfb6d5ebdb579f58244534835f027cd11187ae3
`Should exhaust` - can we assert that instead of adding a dead comment?
where are we adding 2 btc? Could we avoid values from the comments, they're just adding a maintenance burden when we're forgetting updating them
For the record, Knapsack and CoinsBnB are *really* slow (with debug build to avoid all the inlining), making this whole benchmark one of our heaviest - could we reduce some of the iterations?
nit: let's unify the comment styles across the file
Okay, replaced with regular spaces.
I rewrote the paragraph avoiding numbers.
Took your suggestion.
Reduced to ten iterations as you suggested.
Taken
I reduced it to ten targets and added the reserve statement.
I removed the `spendable` arguments.
Nice idea. I realized that the weight was actually -1 everywhere and instead assigned weight and fees as intended.

I switched the minimum and random part as suggested.
I find the straight-up sat amounts more readable than mixing multiple units or the suffix construction, so I left that as was.
Changed to uint32_t
Removed the comment
We currently don’t have access to whether the search concluded or was interrupted early. I propose that we return it with the changes in #32150, and when that gets adopted, we could check here.
I replaced all instances of the space between the comment and value in this document. Please let me know if there was something else that I missed.
Took a variant of the first
Removed the wallet argument from addCoin(…)

Could add helpers to a few more places:
```suggestion
    const CoinEligibilityFilter filter_standard(/*conf_mine=*/1, /*conf_theirs=*/6, /*max_ancestors=*/0);
```

nit: is `/*allow_mixed_output_types=*/false` maybe more accurate here?
We basically always return at https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/3a222507fd4d61c4ac8b4506343a345d66ee76b1/src/wallet/spend.cpp#L716 (is that deliberate?),
which means that
https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/3a222507fd4d61c4ac8b4506343a345d66ee76b1/src/wallet/spend.cpp#L716-L723 is basically never executed.
are these `Copied from` comments useful?

slightly unrelated: we could maybe change floating point here to avoid potential weirdness:

```C++
auto result = AttemptSelection(wallet.chain(), 1002 * COIN + 99 * CENT, group, coin_selection_params, /*allow_mixed_output_types=*/true);
```
or
```C++
auto result = AttemptSelection(wallet.chain(), 100'299'000'000, group, coin_selection_params, /*allow_mixed_output_types=*/true);
```

Given the random targets and random UTXOs, it would be possible for all output types by themselves not to have sufficient funds to produce a solution. In that case, we would fall back to mix the UTXOs and select from all of them together. This is very unlikely with 400 UTXOs, but still seems correct to me.
Removed.

Thanks, looking at the old benchmark again, I realized that I was mistaken in what it did. It had 1000 × 1000 BTC and 1 × 3 BTC, and then looked that 1003 would be selected via 1000 + 3.

I don’t think it’s an interesting case to keep around next to the new benchmark, so I propose removing it.
Taken
Taken
Taken
Put above `det_rand`
Now with weight and fees! :)

> [a7c5615](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33160/changes/a7c56150871c8049a0a5ca3fa47af86b0dca94c5) _bench: Fix type mismatch_:

nit: we could add `#include <cstdint>`

nit2: we could explain in the commit message why this was needed, someting like:
> The BnB helper takes an input index that is passed straight to `COutPoint`.
Use `uint32_t` there so the benchmark does not rely on an implicit narrowing conversion.

> [3b27f0b](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33160/changes/3b27f0b7979adfe1395ab12ef41823d93aebc75f) _bench: Replace Coin Selection bench_:

It seems `group` is mutated during the run (e.g. https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/141fbe4d530b51345e62dee1348e82d8a0406ffc/src/wallet/coinselection.cpp#L114 and https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/fa2670bd4b5b199c417011942228ba87d1613030/src/wallet/coinselection.cpp#L665) so we can't reuse it between runs - since the first iteration would be different from the rest this way.

> [3b27f0b](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33160/changes/3b27f0b7979adfe1395ab12ef41823d93aebc75f) _bench: Replace Coin Selection bench_:

It seems some of the [important parameters](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/0831173c0171de33f95b96324db4041c4799b163/src/wallet/spend.cpp#L858-L860) are not exposed through the constructor (e.g. `m_change_fee`, `min_viable_change`, `m_cost_of_change`, maybe others), we should likely set these as well to have meaningful measurements.
Maybe we could use https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/0831173c0171de33f95b96324db4041c4799b163/src/wallet/spend.cpp#L1063 to set these automatically, not sure.
While we're not actually mixing types in reality, it's not disallowed, just simply not exercised - resolving.
Thanks, that looks great. Taken.
